behavior_summary: When the Lothian corpse object is examined (U8P_LOTHCORP::look event), the script marks the object with object-local info/process-exclude flags and causes the examining process to 'bark' the literal string "Lothian" (i.e., display or speak the name).
The return value from Item::bark is captured and tested with an implication-style opcode; the result is stored into a small temporary and the script then suspends/returns.
All observable effects are local to the object/process interaction—no global script flags are read or written by this handler.
notable_control_flow: Entry on U8P_LOTHCORP::look/examine event -> set object info / process-exclude -> call Item::bark(viewer/process, "Lothian") -> free temporary string -> test bark return (implies) -> store implication result -> suspend/return
The implication opcode and subsequent suspend suggest the bark return may influence whether further processing or notification occurs, but control always reaches the suspend/return after the check.
open_questions: Exact meaning and numeric id of the object info bit set by the handler (opcode shows a set-info but the specific semantic meaning is inferred, not explicit).
Precise semantics of the 'implies' opcode and how its result affects game behavior beyond storing a temporary value and suspending (e.g., whether it gates additional logic elsewhere).
Whether Item::bark's return value indicates success/failure, player-visible acknowledgement, or a queued display result (the script only tests it and does not branch locally).

main_function.name: U8P_LOTHCORP::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_LOTHCORP::look' (examine) event for a Lothian corpse: mark the object with info/process-exclude and make the examining process emit/display the name "Lothian".
entry_conditions: Triggered when an actor/process looks at or examines the LOTHCORP object (the 'U8P_LOTHCORP::look' event).
Invoked with the viewer/process pointer passed in (BP+06h).

function_map.name: U8P_LOTHCORP::look
function_map.role: Examine/inspect event handler for the Lothian corpse object that sets object-local state and requests a bark from the viewer process.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item method used to make a process or item emit/display/say a short text string (here: "Lothian").

external_calls.purpose: Ask the provided process/viewer (BP+06h) to emit/display/say the literal string "Lothian" so the corpse's name is presented to the player/process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the Lothian corpse object is examined (U8P_LOTHCORP::look event), the script marks the object with object-local info/process-exclude flags and causes the examining process to 'bark' the literal string "Lothian" (i.e., display or speak the name).
